title: Methylene blue protects hair follicle stem cells from GLP-1 metabolic stress
description: Aging-US paper from University of Maryland shows methylene blue boosts hair follicle stem cell viability via mitochondrial antioxidant action and β-catenin activation, and crucially protects HFSCs from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death. The finding offers a mechanistic counter-strategy for GLP-1-associated hair thinning now widely reported with semaglutide and tirzepatide use.

title: Sleep apnea drives accelerated vascular aging — clearing p16 cells reverses it
description: New mouse work shows intermittent hypoxia from sleep apnea increases vascular senescence and blood pressure, and that removing p16-expressing senescent cells reverses the dysfunction. This positions senolytics as a candidate adjuvant therapy for the 1+ billion adults with OSA-driven hypertension.

title: Tozorakimab hits Phase 3 primary endpoint in COPD — first IL-33 blocker validated
description: AstraZeneca's tozorakimab met the primary endpoint in the Phase 3 MIRANDA COPD trial, validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for one of the most common diseases of aging. Combined with concurrent BCLXL-PROTAC senolytic preclinical work, COPD is becoming the first major proving ground for age-targeted lung therapeutics.

title: Electromagnetic field cyclic reprogramming extends mouse lifespan
description: New study reports electromagnetic field-induced cyclic cellular reprogramming increased survival and improved multiple age-related tissue features in mice — a fully non-invasive alternative to gene-therapy partial reprogramming. If reproduced, it could open a device-based path to the Sinclair / Yamanaka rejuvenation program that bypasses AAV delivery.

title: Aging-US Volume 18 publishes precision-senolytics review reframing the field
description: A May 15 Aging-US review codifies the precision-senolytics paradigm: senescent cells are heterogeneous and include physiologically essential subsets (embryonic development, wound healing, tissue homeostasis), so next-gen geroprotectors must clear deleterious subpopulations while preserving beneficial ones. This pivot reframes Mayo/Unity/Cellage pipelines and challenges blunt senolytic combo dosing.

title: Methylene Blue May Help Protect Hair Follicle Stem Cells from Aging and Metabolic Stress
description: University of Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced metabolic stress and cell death.

title: Precision Anti-Aging Strategies Aim to Target Harmful Senescent Cells While Preserving Beneficial Ones
description: New Volume 18 review argues senescent cells are not merely deleterious and that subsets play essential roles in embryonic development, wound healing and tissue homeostasis — pushing the field toward precision clearance.

title: Not all Alzheimer's leads to dementia
description: Coverage of evidence that immature hippocampal neurons and their stress-response programs may underlie cognitive resilience in people with substantial Alzheimer's pathology who do not develop dementia.

title: Immunosenescence and Inflammaging as Drivers of Neurodegeneration
description: Review argues that age-related immune dysfunction and chronic inflammation actively drive neurodegenerative disease progression and therapy resistance, with implications for combination immunomodulatory protocols.

title: New Mechanism: Why Ageing Makes Blood Pressure Worse in Sleep Apnea
description: Mouse model shows intermittent hypoxia increases vascular senescence and blood pressure, and that targeted removal of p16-expressing cells reverses the dysfunction — positioning senolytics for OSA-driven hypertension.

title: Scientists Prolong the Life of Mice With Invisible Energy Fields
description: Electromagnetic field-induced cyclic cellular reprogramming in mice increased survival and improved several age-related tissue features — a potential non-invasive analogue to AAV-delivered Yamanaka partial reprogramming.

title: Ultrastructural Signs of High Functional Activity of Neuromuscular Synapses in Aging Rats After Photobiomodulation
description: Photobiomodulation improved neuromuscular junction structure and increased functional activity markers in aged rat muscle, suggesting a low-cost device-based intervention for age-related neuromuscular decline.

title: Small-molecule IGF1R inhibitors extend healthspan in a mouse model
description: Mouse study reports IGF1R inhibitors improved several healthspan measures, though side effects limited enthusiasm for the tested compounds — adds to the case for IGF1 pathway modulation in healthspan.

title: Adulthood depletion of Integrator extends lifespan and healthspan via defective pre-mRNA processing
description: Disrupting Integrator complex function in adult C. elegans extended lifespan and healthspan via altered RNA processing and induced mitohormetic mitochondrial gene expression changes.

title: Older Mice May Offer New Insight Into Cancer and Aging
description: Melanoma study shows cancer spread varied with mouse age and implicates γδ T cells and tumor immune evasion — relevant to using more representative aged cancer models.

title: How Nature Tunes Aging: group-living mammals live longer
description: Comparative study across mammal species finds group-living and pair-living species live longer than solitary species, adding evidence for social structure as a longevity driver after controlling for body mass.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal antibody hit primary endpoint in MIRANDA Phase 3 COPD trial, validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for one of the most common diseases of aging.

title: Insilico forms longevity board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico Medicine stands up a dedicated Longevity Board to oversee its AI-driven aging research program, formalizing its commitment to geroscience translation alongside the recent Eli Lilly $2.75B deal.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, its NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program, supporting further development in cardiometabolic and inflammaging indications.

title: AlzeCure completes Phase Ib study of ACD856
description: AlzeCure Pharma completes Phase Ib study of ACD856, a TrkA/TrkB positive modulator for Alzheimer's and cognitive disorders.

title: AB Science links masitinib to survival benefit in ALS trial
description: AB Science reports masitinib showed a survival benefit in an ALS trial, supporting tyrosine kinase modulation as an approach for neurodegeneration.

title: GSK acquires 35Pharma in $950M deal
description: GSK acquires 35Pharma for $950M to advance its pulmonary hypertension drug — significant validation for the aging-related cardiopulmonary therapeutics segment.

title: Osteoboost raises $8M to scale FDA-cleared prescription bone wearable
description: Esplanade Ventures-led $8M raise expands access to Osteoboost's FDA-cleared vibration therapy device for osteopenia and osteoporosis, already prescribed by 2,500+ physicians.

title: Inbrain completes enrollment in first human graphene brain study
description: Inbrain Neuroelectronics completes enrollment in its first human graphene brain-computer interface study, advancing neuromodulation device class with implications for neurodegeneration.

title: Biotechs race to turn aging science into cell-based therapies
description: Feature on biotechs translating aging biology into autologous and allogeneic cell therapies, from senescent-cell-clearing CAR-T to iPSC rejuvenation platforms.

title: Aging Immune Systems Show Reduced Ability to Clear Tuberculosis During Treatment
description: New Volume 18 Aging-US paper shows aging immune systems have reduced bacterial clearance capacity during TB treatment, with implications for geriatric infectious-disease management.

title: Can We Reverse Aging? — The Daily on cellular rejuvenation
description: NYT Daily / Sunday Daily podcast features Susan Dominus on cellular rejuvenation research, the scientists involved, and the financial backers funding the longevity sector now part of a $2T global wellness industry.

title: Diet rewires biological age in just 4 weeks — Sydney Aging Cell study
description: University of Sydney trial in adults 65-75 found 4 weeks of lower-fat or more plant-based protein diets reduced estimated biological age; strongest signal from the lower-fat, higher-carb arm.

title: Large rapamycin clinical trial launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: NIA-funded multi-phase study of rapamycin and everolimus in healthy adults 65-90 launches at UT San Antonio, structured to define optimal anti-aging dose ahead of a 6-month randomized placebo-controlled trial in 84 participants.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026: SRN-901, BCLXL-PROTAC, Tomatidine
description: April roundup highlights Seragon's SRN-901 extending median remaining lifespan 33% in 18-month-old mice, BCLXL-PROTAC for COPD, and Tomatidine as a senotherapeutic improving cognition in aged mice.
